title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During afternoon tea at Rutherford Hall, Dr. Campbell arrives to find Emma has prepared a birthday cake for him. Miss Marple, who is recovering from a cold, joins the group. During the meal, Mr. Cruncher makes jokes regarding poisoned fish paste, prompting Cedric to demonstrate the food's safety by swallowing a sandwich whole. However, Miss Marple begins to choke on a fish bone while eating a sandwich, and Dr. Campbell uses his professional skills with tweezers to examine her throat.

When Mrs. McGillicuddy enters the room, she recognizes Dr. Campbell as the man she saw on the train. Miss Marple seizes this moment to trap him, claiming that Mrs. McGillicuddy is an eyewitness to his murder of a woman on a parallel train. Although Campbell attempts to intimidate her, Miss Marple reveals that Mrs. McGillicuddy had already reported the killer's features to the police on December 20th.

Miss Marple exposes Campbell's true identity as Comber and details his motives. He killed his wife, Anna Strandwasser, to marry the wealthy Emma without the risk of bigamy. To cover his tracks, he hid her body in a warehouse coffin and used various deceptions, including a fake identity and a postcard from Jamaica. Miss Marple also reveals that he attempted to poison the Crickenshaw family with small doses of arsenic to facilitate his greed. Miss Marple admits she orchestrated the confrontation by tricking Campbell into believing Mrs. McGillicuddy had seen his face, when in reality, the woman had only seen his back. The encounter concludes with Miss Marple expressing hope that Emma will find a better man than Campbell.
